title: Markov Chains, Conceptually
concept: Each noising step depends only on the previous step's state, forming what's called a Markov chain — a memoryless sequence of small changes. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: The Reverse Diffusion Process.'

exercises.prompt: When you take one step forward in a game, does your NEXT step depend on every step you've EVER taken, or just where you are RIGHT NOW?

options.text: Just where you are right now

exercises.explain: Exactly! You only need to know your current spot to take the next step — you don't need the whole history. That's the core idea behind today's topic!

cards.title: One Step at a Time
cards.body: Imagine crossing a bridge made of stepping stones. To know where you'll land next, you only need to know which stone you're standing on RIGHT NOW — not every stone you stepped on before. Diffusion models noise images the exact same way!

cards.title: Markov Chain
cards.body: A Markov chain is a sequence of steps where each new step depends ONLY on the current state — not on the full history before it. In diffusion, each noisier image is created using only the previous noisy image, one small change at a time.

cards.title: The Forgetful Board Game
cards.body: Think of a board game where your next move only depends on which square you're on now — not how you got there. Roll the dice, move from THIS square. The path you took to get here doesn't matter. Markov chains are 'memoryless' in exactly this way.

cards.title: Noising, Step by Step
cards.body: Step 5 of noising an image only looks at Step 4's image and adds a little more noise. It doesn't need to remember Step 1, 2, or 3 — Step 4 already contains everything needed to make Step 5. Each step is a small, independent nudge forward.

cards.title: Myth: The Model Remembers Everything
cards.body:
Myth: Each noising step must recall the entire history of every previous step to work correctly.

Truth: It only needs the PREVIOUS state! This 'memoryless' property is what makes Markov chains simple, efficient, and easy to reverse later.

cards.title: Memory Needed vs. Memoryless
cards.body: Compare a process that needs full history to one that only needs the current state.

left.text: Must remember every past step to decide the next one — slow and complicated

right.text: Only looks at the current step to decide the next one — simple and fast

exercises.prompt: In a Markov chain, what does each step depend on?

options.text: Every state that came before it

exercises.explain: Correct! A Markov chain is 'memoryless' — each step only needs to know the current state, not the whole past.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In diffusion's noising process, Step 10 needs to know exactly what Step 1 looked like.
exercises.explain: False! Step 10 only needs Step 9's noisy image. It doesn't need to look back at Step 1 at all — that's the memoryless magic of a Markov chain.

exercises.sentence: A Markov chain is a sequence of steps where each step depends only on the ___ state.
exercises.explain: Each step only looks at the state right before it — the 'previous' state — to determine the next one.

exercises.prompt: Why is it useful for diffusion models that each noising step only depends on the previous step?

options.text: It makes the process simple and easy to reverse later
It makes the model forget how to generate images
It requires storing every past image forever

exercises.explain: Right! Being memoryless keeps each step small and simple, which makes it much easier to later reverse the process and recover the original image.

exercises.prompt: A friend says: 'To predict the next noisy image in diffusion, the model must look at ALL previous noisy images from the very start.' Is this correct?

options.text: No — it only needs the single previous state, since this is a Markov chain
Yes — more history always means better noise predictions
No — it needs no information at all, not even the previous state
Yes — but only for the first 5 steps

exercises.explain: Correct! Diffusion's noising process is a Markov chain — each step depends only on the immediately previous state, not the full history. This memoryless property keeps things simple. Next up: The Reverse Diffusion Process.
